title: Block-bootstrap / HAC Sharpe confidence interval (Ledoit-Wolf) - diagnostic
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: Our sqrt(trades_per_year) annualization and any iid-Sharpe SE ignore serial correlation in the PnL stream; Ledoit-Wolf (2008) HAC + stationary/circular block bootstrap is the canonical fix. quant-phd-advisor: fixes a real inference defect but largely OVERLAPS PSR (already probabilistic, non-normal-aware) and inherits the same small-sample liability as CPCV/DSR on <30-trade folds.
value_rationale: VERDICT: Adopt-with-caveats, LOW priority. Marginal value over existing PSR is modest; lowest value-for-effort of the validation group.
scope: Use arch library's stationary-bootstrap Sharpe CI as a DIAGNOSTIC on aggregated OOF PnL, NOT a per-fold gate
CRITICAL: HAC/bootstrap inference over-rejects at small samples — a <30-trade fold is exactly the failure regime
recommendation: Adopt as a cheap arch-based diagnostic, not a gate. Lowest priority of the validation items.
out_of_scope: Not a per-fold gate (small-sample liberal inference). Not a replacement for PSR.

external_refs.label: Ledoit & Wolf 2008 — robust Sharpe hypothesis testing

history.from: Block-bootstrap / HAC Sharpe confidence interval (Ledoit-Wolf) — diagnostic
